message:
fix(security): four hardening fixes across TLS, SCRAM, DNS, and gRPC

Four independent security fixes surfaced during audit batches 496-503:

1. database/postgres.rs — SCRAM constant-time comparison used
   short-circuit && for the length check, allowing a timing
   side-channel where differing lengths would skip the byte-by-byte
   content scan entirely.  Fix: use bitwise & so both the length
   comparison and content scan always execute in constant time.

2. tls/connector.rs — Building a TlsConnector with an empty root
   certificate store previously emitted a warning log but continued,
   producing a connector that could never verify any server
   certificate.  Fix: promote to a hard Err, making it impossible
   to construct a TlsConnector that silently accepts all certificates.

3. net/dns/resolver.rs — DNS answer pre-allocation used
   Vec::with_capacity(answer_count) directly from the wire, allowing
   a malicious DNS response with a large answer_count to trigger
   excessive memory allocation.  Fix: cap pre-allocation to 512
   entries (well above any legitimate DNS response).

4. grpc/web.rs — A missing grpc-status trailer was interpreted as
   code 0 (OK), silently treating protocol violations as success.
   Fix: default to code 13 (INTERNAL) when the status trailer is
   absent, correctly surfacing the protocol error to callers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
